author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Replaced the promote-to-complex-then-truncate path in ferray-fft with dedicated real-to-complex / complex-to-real transforms from the realfft crate.

Dependencies:
- Added realfft 3.4 (resolved to 3.5.0) as a workspace dep. realfft is built directly on rustfft so no new transitive deps — pulls only what rustfft already did.

ferray-fft/src/plan.rs:
- Added a second LazyLock<Mutex<RealPlanCache>> alongside the existing complex cache. RealPlanCache holds a realfft::RealFftPlanner<f64> plus two HashMaps keyed by transform size: one for RealToComplex<f64> (forward) and one for ComplexToReal<f64> (inverse). The real cache is lazy — only constructed on first rfft/irfft use, so pure complex FFT users pay no cost.
- Public helpers get_cached_real_forward(size) and get_cached_real_inverse(size).

ferray-fft/src/nd.rs:
- New pub(crate) rfft_along_axis and irfft_along_axis helpers that mirror the complex fft_along_axis architecture but call through the real plans directly.
- Both use the par_chunks_mut pattern from #433 — a single contiguous output buffer partitioned across rayon workers, with per-thread input/scratch buffers via for_each_init. No per-lane allocations.
- realfft's process_with_scratch writes directly into each worker's assigned output chunk; final step scatters chunks into the strided output.

ferray-fft/src/real.rs:
- rfft() delegates to rfft_along_axis: materializes the input as &[f64] once, calls the lane helper, wraps the result. No more real_to_complex_flat + full complex FFT + truncate_hermitian.
- irfft() delegates to irfft_along_axis: passes the complex input directly, lets realfft handle the Hermitian unfolding internally. No more extend_hermitian.
- rfftn_impl switched to NumPy's order: rfft along the LAST axis first (producing the folded representation), then complex fft on axes[0..last-1] in order. Previously the whole pipeline ran complex FFTs and truncated at the end.
- irfftn_impl similarly: complex inverse FFTs on axes[0..last-1] first, then irfft on the last axis.
- Deleted the now-dead truncate_hermitian, extend_hermitian, compute_strides, flat_to_multi helpers (152 lines).

5 new tests as regression guards for the realfft path:
1. rfft_single_cosine_matches_analytical — verifies a pure cosine at frequency k produces a single n/2+0j spike at bin k and ~0 elsewhere.
2. rfft_parseval_holds_for_multi_lane — 4×16 multi-lane array; verifies sum|x[i]|^2 * n == sum|X[k]|^2 for the Hermitian-folded representation (bins 0 and n/2 count once; interior bins count twice for their unstored conjugates).
3. rfft_odd_length_impulse — 5-point delta → all 3 bins == 1+0j.
4. rfft_multi_lane_with_zero_padding — 3 lanes of length 2 padded to 8 → output shape (3, 5) with correct DC values.
5. irfft_multi_lane_axis0_roundtrip — 6×4 array, rfft/irfft along axis 0 exercising the strided-lane scatter path for both directions.

Validation — the critical correctness check is the existing oracle_rfft and oracle_irfft tests which compare ferray output to NumPy reference fixtures. Both still pass with the new realfft-backed implementation.

Performance: theoretically ~2× faster for rfft since realfft uses a half-size complex FFT internally instead of a full-size one, and avoids the extra truncation step. Not benchmarked as part of this commit.

Result: 71 ferray-fft lib tests pass (was 66, +5). Oracle tests + property tests (including prop_rfft_irfft_roundtrip and prop_real_input_conjugate_symmetry) pass unchanged. Workspace tests + ferray-fft clippy clean. Closes #432.
